summary: Adds tools/sync_progress_metadata.py as the single canonical sync/check command for README + tools/decomp_work/progress.json derived from report.json, wires it into check_report_sanity.py so staleness now fails the existing sanity gate, and refreshes progress.json from 11 stale entries to 3,432 real actively-completed functions (excluding asm wrappers/stubs).
decomp_lessons: tools/decomp_work/progress.json had drifted badly stale (only 11 completed entries recorded vs 3,432 actual real-C completed functions in report.json) -- progress bookkeeping files can silently rot when agents update report.json/README directly without also updating progress.json.

Completed-function tracking must explicitly exclude asm wrappers and stubs, counting only active real-C matched functions, to avoid inflating campaign progress metrics with unconverted asm-wrapper stubs that merely happen to byte-match.
smart_moves: Made check_report_sanity.py itself call `sync_progress_metadata.py --check` as a subprocess when checking the committed root report.json, so metadata staleness fails the existing CI-style sanity gate without needing a separate workflow file change.

Wired the new sync command through all existing auto-update paths (surgical_report_update.py, update_report.py, auto_push.sh, report_cadence.py) in one PR so no code path could bypass the new canonical sync and silently re-introduce drift.
auto_push.sh now commits both README.md and tools/decomp_work/progress.json together (previously only README.md), preventing progress.json from falling out of sync again during unattended pushes.

key_files.role: New canonical --sync/--check command deriving README progress table and tools/decomp_work/progress.json completed list from report.json.
Added a check that, when validating the committed root report.json, also runs sync_progress_metadata.py --check and fails sanity if metadata is stale.
Regenerated from 11 stale entries to 3,432 active real-C completed function entries derived from current report.json.
Auto-push loop now runs sync_progress_metadata.py --sync and commits README.md + progress.json together instead of README.md alone.
